compiled_state.title: test-batched.sh: resume cycles with --timeout=50 -> exits 144 (SIGURG) despite time-budget design
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: test-batched.sh is specifically designed to exit before the ~73s tool timeout ceiling by tracking elapsed time and yielding control. However, during sprint execution of epic 0d66-ceb6, multiple consecutive invocations of test-batched.sh (with state file resume) exited with code 144 (SIGURG). This suggests either: (1) the internal time tracking underestimates actual elapsed time, (2) individual test files exceed the per-test budget without the wrapper detecting it, or (3) the 50s timeout parameter is too close to the ~73s ceiling when combined with startup overhead. Reproduction: run test-batched.sh --timeout=50 --runner=bash --test-dir=tests/scripts in a worktree with 227+ test files. Observe exit 144 on multiple resume cycles.

comments.body:
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Identify root cause of test-batched.sh exiting with code 144 during resume cycles
- [ ] Fix the time budget calculation or test timeout handling so individual tests don't exceed the tool timeout ceiling
- [ ] Verify: Run test-batched.sh --timeout=50 --runner=bash --test-dir=tests/scripts and confirm no exit 144 over at least 200 test completions
- [ ] No regression in existing test-batched.sh tests (tests/scripts/test-test-batched.sh passes)
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
CHECKPOINT 3/6: Root cause identified ✓

ROOT CAUSE (two compounding issues):

1. **State-loading O(N) python3 subprocess per entry**: test-batched.sh lines 338-345 call  once per entry in COMPLETED_LIST to check if the result is 'interrupted'. With 258 entries = 258 python3 spawns = ~15.9s overhead.

2. **Path normalization O(N) subshells per file**: bash-runner.sh runs 2 subshell calls (cd + pwd) per discovered test file to compute absolute paths. With 260 files = 520 subshell spawns = ~6.1s overhead.

Combined overhead: ~22s BEFORE _bash_runner_run's START_TIME is set.
With --timeout=50: internal kill fires at T+72s (22+50).
Claude Code SIGURG fires at T+73s.
Race window: only 1 second.

FIX: 
1. Parse the results JSON once in Python and filter interrupted tests in a single pass - no per-entry subprocess calls.
2. Hoist _abs_test_dir computation outside the per-file loop in bash-runner.sh.
3. Bonus: add a startup-overhead budget to ensure internal timeout fires with sufficient margin before 73s ceiling.
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
CHECKPOINT 4/6: Implementation complete ✓

FIXES APPLIED:

**Fix 1** (plugins/dso/scripts/test-batched.sh, lines ~329-346):
Replaced O(N) python3 subprocess-per-entry interrupted-check with a single python3 call that parses the entire state file and emits results + completed IDs in one pass. Eliminates 258 python3 spawns with 258 completed entries (~15.9s overhead eliminated).

**Fix 2** (plugins/dso/scripts/runners/bash-runner.sh, lines ~118-126):  
Hoisted _abs_test_dir computation outside the per-file loop. Previously computed via subshell /Users/joeoakhart/digital-service-orchestra-worktrees/worktree-20260406-104322 on every iteration = O(N) subshells. Now computed once before the loop. Eliminates half of path normalization overhead (~3s reduction with 260 files).

BEFORE: 258-completed resume took 17.6s overhead + tests. Exit 144 with --timeout=50.
AFTER: 258-completed resume takes 4-8s total. Exit 0.

**New failing test added** (tests/scripts/test-test-batched.sh):
test_state_loading_overhead_is_sublinear: creates 200 completed + 1 remaining, verifies full run completes in <10s. Was failing (took 10+s) before fix, passes (3s) after.
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
CHECKPOINT 5/6: Tests complete ✓

test-test-batched.sh: PASSED 65, FAILED 0

New test test_state_loading_overhead_is_sublinear:
- Was FAILING before fix (10+s with 200 completed entries)
- Now PASSES (3s with 200 completed entries, well under 10s threshold)

All 64 pre-existing tests continue to pass (no regression).
comments.author: Test

comments.body:
CHECKPOINT 6/6: Done ✓

SELF-CHECK PASSED:
- Root cause addressed: O(N) python3 subprocess calls eliminated from state loading
- Interrupted test re-run behavior preserved (regression test passed)
- 258-completed resume: 17.6s → 4-8s total (well within 73s-50s=23s safety window)
- AC criteria all met
- No new out-of-scope bugs found requiring tickets
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Classification: behavioral. Score: severity=1 (functional degradation) + complexity=1 (multiple code paths) + environment=1 (CI failure) + intermittent=1 (env-specific) = 4. Routing: INTERMEDIATE investigation.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: CHECKPOINT 5/6: Fix implemented. Root cause: bash runner used its own START_TIME from _bash_runner_run() instead of the global _SCRIPT_ENTRY_TIME from script entry. Startup overhead (state parsing, test discovery, path canonicalization for 227+ files) was not accounted for in the time budget. Fix: (1) added _SCRIPT_ENTRY_TIME at script entry, (2) bash runner now uses _SCRIPT_ENTRY_TIME for elapsed calculations, (3) added process group kill for orphan cleanup.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Fix: bash runner now uses global _SCRIPT_ENTRY_TIME for elapsed calculations, added process group kill.
comments.author: Test
